title: Community Learning Centers
summary: After-school learning centers in 12 villages giving children consistent access to tutoring, books and a safe place to study.
detail: Children in many of the communities we work with attend overcrowded schools with little individual attention. Our learning centers run six days a week, staffed by trained local tutors, and provide structured help with core subjects, reading time, and exam preparation. Each center also runs a small library and hosts parent-engagement evenings.

title: Girls' Scholarship Program
summary: Scholarships and mentorship for girls at risk of dropping out after primary school.
detail: Girls in our partner communities are statistically far more likely than boys to leave school after class 8. This program covers school fees, uniforms and transport, paired with a mentor who checks in monthly and helps families navigate the path to secondary school and beyond.

title: Vocational Skill Development
summary: Short, practical courses in tailoring, computer literacy and basic bookkeeping for young adults and women.
detail: Many young people in our communities finish school with no direct path to employment. These six-to-twelve week courses are run with local partners and end with placement support or micro-enterprise seed grants for graduates who want to start their own work.
impact: 640 graduates, 70% in paid work within 6 months

title: Child Health & Nutrition Camps
summary: Monthly health check-ups, deworming and nutrition supplementation for children under 12.
detail: Run in partnership with local clinics and volunteer doctors, these camps screen for common deficiencies, provide deworming tablets, and distribute nutrition supplements to flagged cases, with referrals for anything that needs follow-up care.
impact: 5,200 children screened in the last year

title: Emergency Relief Response
summary: Rapid-response distribution of food, water and essentials during floods and other local emergencies.
detail: When flooding or other emergencies hit our operating districts, our volunteer network mobilizes within 48 hours to distribute essentials and coordinate with local authorities on relief camps.

title: Elder Support Circles
summary: Weekly check-in visits and basic healthcare access support for isolated elderly residents.
detail: A newer pilot program pairing volunteers with elderly residents who live alone, providing companionship, help accessing pensions and healthcare, and basic home-safety checks.
impact: 60 elders supported, expanding to 3 new villages in 2026
